revision.comment: 国府神社の概要について説明した。
revision.text: 国府神社は、奈良県(大和国)高市郡高取町下土佐にある神社である。大和国の総社であり、大神神社とともに、大和国内にある寺では別格の地位を占め、人々に信仰されている。

revision.comment: カテゴリ追加等

revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
{{Disambiguation}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kō Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines with the same name]] [[Category:original pages]]

revision.comment: adding new pages tag because that is one I use for finding new links
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
{{Disambiguation}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:new pages]][[category:original pages]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
{{Disambiguation}}

revision.comment: deciding to rename this category to a more descriptive one
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
{{Disambiguation}}

revision.text:
'''Kō ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ō]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[, it en]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[s {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
{{Disambiguation}}

'''Kō ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ō]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[, it en]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[s {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
{{Disambiguation}}

* [[Kō ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:kkk]] [[, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:kkk]] [[) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:kkk]] [[|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient ]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ō]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:kkk]] [[, it en]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:kkk]] [[s {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.

'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.

revision.comment: added [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
{{Disambiguation}}

revision.comment: fixing this horrible error
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]][[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]]{{disambiguation}}

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] to [[Category:unlisted stubs]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]][[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]]{{disambiguation}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:not stubs]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]][[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]]{{disambiguation}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Not stubs]] to [[Category:not stubs]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]][[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]]{{disambiguation}}

'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]][[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]]{{disambiguation}}

revision.comment: some pages here lack the tag but to my knowledge everything here eitherr is an original page or at least worth preserving while I potentially trirm the wikipedia derived articles
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]][[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]]{{disambiguation}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Shrines with the same name]] to [[Category:同名の神社]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]][[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]]{{disambiguation}}

'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:mass bump]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]][[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]]{{disambiguation}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Shrines with identical names]] to [[Category:kk]][[simple:user:immanuelle/{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[category:kk]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]][[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]]{{disambiguation}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:bump-01-18]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]][[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]]{{disambiguation}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]][[Category:Improperly tagged disambiguation pages]]{{disambiguation}}

revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.

revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]]{{disambiguation}}

revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en}} as its deity.
[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]]{{disambiguation}}

revision.text:
'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa Shrine|qq=}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikawa, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} as its deity.
{{disambiguation}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:国府神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:国府神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:国府神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q108778882]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q108778882]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori, Nara|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa Shrine|qq=}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikawa, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} as its deity.
{{disambiguation}}
{{draft categories|

'''Kō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|国府神社}})
* [[Kō Shrine (Takatori)]] - Located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. This shrine, situated in Shitakusa, Takatori, is the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}. It holds a distinguished position along with {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa Shrine|qq=}}, being a central focus of faith and worship within the province. * [[Kō Shrine (Ichikawa)]] - Found in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikawa, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}, this ancient shrine was founded on February 8, 1087 (Eihō 7). Also known as Ōtori Ōkami, it enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} as its deity.
{{disambiguation}}
